steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a verb tense question. The main clause “An emulsifier is a type of compound that ______” uses simple present tense to state a timeless definition. The relative clause filling the blank must describe the compound’s general, ongoing function — so the verb must also be simple present to stay consistent.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘served’: Simple past tense; implies the function ended in the past, which contradicts the timeless definition being stated. Tense consistency rule violated. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘had served’: Past perfect tense; implies the function was completed before some other past event, making no sense in a general definition. Tense consistency rule violated. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘serves’: Simple present tense; matches “is” in the main clause and correctly describes an ongoing, general characteristic of emulsifiers. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘was serving’: Past progressive tense; implies an action in progress at a specific past moment, which is inappropriate for a scientific definition of a compound’s function. Tense consistency rule violated. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
